problem.title: Number of 1 Bits
problem.description: Write a function that takes an unsigned integer and returns the number of '1' bits it has (also known as the Hamming weight).

example.explanation: 1011 has three '1' bits. Use n & (n-1) to clear the lowest set bit repeatedly.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Bit Manipulation [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Repeatedly check the least significant bit with n & 1, increment the count if it is 1, then logical-right-shift n by 1 to process the next bit. Using unsigned right shift ensures the sign bit is treated as data rather than sign extension. The loop terminates in at most 32 iterations regardless of value, making this effectively O(1).
